Class AbstractInstruction

java.lang.Object
dk.tij.registermaschine.core.instructions.api.AbstractInstruction
Direct Known Subclasses:
AdditionInstruction, DivisionInstruction, HaltInstruction, InputInstruction, JumpInstruction, MoveInstruction, MultiplicationInstruction, OutputInstruction, SubtractionInstruction
public abstract class AbstractInstruction extends Object
Base class for all instruction implementations in the Regisermaschine.

An AbstractInstruction defines the executable behaviour of a machine instruction. Concrete subclasses implement the executeInstruction(IExecutionContext, ICompiledOperand[]) method to perform the specific oepration.

Each instruction instance is associated with:

  • An opcode identifying the instruction in compiled programs
  • A fixed number of operands
  • An optional execution ICondition

The Executor retrieves instruction handlers from the instruction set and invokes them during program execution.

Before execution, the instruction may validate its operands and evaluate its execution condition.

Since:
1.0.0
Author:
TiJ

  • Field Details

    • OpCode

      public final byte OpCode

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractInstruction

      public AbstractInstruction(byte opcode, int operandCount, ICondition condition)
      Creates a new instruction definition.
      Parameters:
      opcode - the numeric opcode identifying the instruction
      operandCount - the number of operands required by this instruction
      condition - an optional condition controlling whether the instruction should execute, or null if the instruction should always execute

  • Method Details

    • validate

      public void validate(ICompiledOperand[] operands)
      Validates the operand array supplied to this instruction.

      The default implementation verifies that the operand array exists and contains at least the expected number of operands.

      Subclasses may override this method if additional validation rules are required.

      Parameters:
      operands - the operands supplied for execution
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the operand count is insufficient

    • shouldExecute

      public boolean shouldExecute(IExecutionContext context)
      Determines whether this instruction should execute in the current context.

      If a condition was provided during construction, the condition is evaluated against the IExecutionContext. If no condition is present, the instruction will always execute.

      Parameters:
      context - the current execution context
      Returns:
      true if the instruction should execute

    • executeInstruction

      public abstract void executeInstruction(IExecutionContext context, ICompiledOperand[] operands)
      Executes the instruction logic.

      This method is called by the runtime executor when the instruction is reached during program execution.

      Parameters:
      context - the execution context containing the machine state
      operands - the compiled operands supplied ot the instruction

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representing of the instruction definition.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a formatted string containing the opcode and operand count
